title: [Feature]: Add payload presence metadata to NatsEnvelope
problem: The current envelope safely represents missing or empty message data as bytes, but it cannot distinguish a producer-sent empty payload from a payload intentionally omitted by a headers-only JetStream consumer. That ambiguity weakens audit records, DLQ explanations, and payload-hash idempotency fallback.
proposal: Add a backward-compatible payload-presence contract to NatsEnvelope and the standard metadata snapshot. The core should detect headers-only messages through the NATS-provided message-size header, keep data as bytes for API compatibility, and expose explicit fields such as payload_present, payload_omitted, payload_omitted_reason, and original_payload_size_bytes. The contract should reject unsafe payload-hash fallback when payload bytes were omitted and no stronger idempotency key is available.
users: Sink implementers, auditors reviewing custody records, and operators who need to prove whether a destination stored the original body or a metadata-only representation.
delivery_semantics: Payload-presence metadata is descriptive and must not allow sinks to ACK messages. It must support existing commit-then-acknowledge handling and permanent-failure routing when an idempotency strategy is unsafe for omitted payloads.
security: The metadata must not include the original payload. Message size can still be sensitive, so documentation must treat original_payload_size_bytes as operational metadata and keep it out of high-cardinality metric labels by default.
acceptance: NatsEnvelope exposes explicit payload-presence metadata without breaking existing data access.
The standard metadata JSON document records payload presence, omission reason, and original size when available.
Headers-only payload-hash fallback is rejected or documented as unavailable.
Tests cover normal payloads, producer-empty payloads, headers-only omitted payloads, malformed size headers, and missing metadata.
Public API compatibility tests are updated.
tests: Add unit tests for envelope construction, metadata snapshots, idempotency key selection, payload normalization, malformed Nats-Msg-Size values, and public API compatibility. Run scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update docs/headers-only-delivery.md, docs/sink-framework.md, docs/idempotency.md, docs/configuration.md, docs/security.md, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close after the release containing the payload-presence envelope contract is published. Include release tag, public API notes, tests run, and migration guidance.
